question.text: When the price of the underlying commodity rises, the impact on option premiums is:

answer.text: Call premium rises, Put premium falls
Both call and put premiums rise
Call premium falls, Put premium rises
Both call and put premiums fall

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rising underlying price increases call value (more likely to be exercised profitably) and decreases put value (less likely to be exercised). **The Trap:** The impact is opposite for calls and puts. They don't move in the same direction when the underlying price changes. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII and I cover the same directional impact analysis. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is why delta is positive for calls and negative for puts — delta captures the directional sensitivity of option price to underlying price movement.
